sections.Description:
Tumblebugs attack by summoning anUnstable Boulderthat will explode when damaged, then threatening to damage it and inflict 1 damage on all of its adjancent tiles, including the Tumblebug. These boulders do not explode if they are not damaged. If a Tumblebug is forced to attack a mech directly it will not summon an unstable boulder.
As a rare Vek, Tumblebugs cannot appear on the first island and are restricted to two spawns per mission, including itsalpha. They cannot spawn on missions withexplosive mines.
sections.Strategy:
Tumblebugs are notorious for either placing their Unstable Boulders next to many buildings at once and blocking grounded Mech movement, or in an open space where it will not damage anything significant. If they do the former, try to push the Tumblebug away from the boulder or eliminate it with a strong attack;Smokingthem can be effective as well since it will discourage them from attacking near their Unstable Boulders in future turns, which could otherwise make them difficult to push away. If they do the latter, you can move your Mechs out of the way to focus on more dangerous Vek and let it damage itself; or if you can spare an action, use weapons that damage and push the Tumblebug into its own boulder, which will cause additional bump damage and explosive decay damage.
When deploying against Tumblebugs, try to encourage them to put their Unstable Boulders in an open space next to your Mechs; much likeBlobbers, they will attempt to attack as many targets as possible, so offer them two Mechs to attack with no buildings around if it does not compromise positioning.
Even after the Tumblebug is eliminated, Unstable Boulders will remain around and become a nuisance to deal with, so make sure to move them away from grid buildings and shield or freeze them so that they endure enough hits to not explode. Note that Tumblebugs and other enemy Vek will not attempt to target explosive boulders again (see Figure A and Figure B); however, enemies with multi-target range likeCentipedesorAlpha Hornetscan additionally target Unstable Boulders, if they will also target a Mech or a building with another part of their attack range.
It's worth putting off an island with Tumblebugs until later in the timeline if you don't think your current weapon set is sufficient to neutralize them. In particular, be very careful with Tumblebugs in missions with environmental effects, such asAir StrikesfromArchive, Inc.orConveyor BeltsfromDetritus Disposal, as these effects can also destroy or place boulders in inconvenient spots.

full_text:
Tumblebug
Details
Health
3
Move
3
Systems
Explosive Prize
(
Enemy
)
Effect
Create an explosive boulder, preparing to destroy it next turn by attacking it for 1 damage.
The
Tumblebug
is a rare
Vek
from
Into The Breach
introduced in the Advanced Edition. They are only present if Advanced Enemies are turned on at the start of a new timeline.
Description
[
]
Tumblebugs attack by summoning an
Unstable Boulder
that will explode when damaged, then threatening to damage it and inflict 1 damage on all of its adjancent tiles, including the Tumblebug. These boulders do not explode if they are not damaged. If a Tumblebug is forced to attack a mech directly it will not summon an unstable boulder.
As a rare Vek, Tumblebugs cannot appear on the first island and are restricted to two spawns per mission, including its
alpha
. They cannot spawn on missions with
explosive mines
.
Strategy
[
]
Tumblebugs are notorious for either placing their Unstable Boulders next to many buildings at once and blocking grounded Mech movement, or in an open space where it will not damage anything significant. If they do the former, try to push the Tumblebug away from the boulder or eliminate it with a strong attack;
Smoking
them can be effective as well since it will discourage them from attacking near their Unstable Boulders in future turns, which could otherwise make them difficult to push away. If they do the latter, you can move your Mechs out of the way to focus on more dangerous Vek and let it damage itself; or if you can spare an action, use weapons that damage and push the Tumblebug into its own boulder, which will cause additional bump damage and explosive decay damage.
When deploying against Tumblebugs, try to encourage them to put their Unstable Boulders in an open space next to your Mechs; much like
Blobbers
, they will attempt to attack as many targets as possible, so offer them two Mechs to attack with no buildings around if it does not compromise positioning.
Figure A: Tumblebug at C4 is attacking its own explosive boulder. The
Defense Mech
has shielded it with its action, so it will not explode.
Even after the Tumblebug is eliminated, Unstable Boulders will remain around and become a nuisance to deal with, so make sure to move them away from grid buildings and shield or freeze them so that they endure enough hits to not explode. Note that Tumblebugs and other enemy Vek will not attempt to target explosive boulders again (see Figure A and Figure B); however, enemies with multi-target range like
Centipedes
or
Alpha Hornets
can additionally target Unstable Boulders, if they will also target a Mech or a building with another part of their attack range.
Figure B: In the next turn, the C4 Tumblebug has no valid targets, so it skips its action (note that it is shown as "Not Attacking")
It's worth putting off an island with Tumblebugs until later in the timeline if you don't think your current weapon set is sufficient to neutralize them. In particular, be very careful with Tumblebugs in missions with environmental effects, such as
Air Strikes
from
Archive, Inc.
or
Conveyor Belts
from
Detritus Disposal
, as these effects can also destroy or place boulders in inconvenient spots.
